mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery D250 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Discovery D250 (AJ20D6) engine typically costs £4,500–£7,000 supply only. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery D250 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the AJ20D6 straight-six is a major reliability upgrade over the older V6 SDV6. It has no known crankshaft bearing failures. However, it is a modern Euro 6d diesel with complex emissions hardware. AdBlue system reliability (NOx sensors, injectors, crystallisation) is the primary area of concern.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery D250?

acceptedAnswer.text: AdBlue/SCR system failures (NOx sensors, crystallisation), cooling system leaks (plastic pipes), and DPF-related oil dilution (short urban journeys). All are manageable with proper maintenance and are not catastrophic mechanical failures.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery D250 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-maintained Discovery 5 (values £25,000–£45,000+), a £5,500–£6,000 reconditioned engine is 15–25% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a solid car. The Land Rover scrap threshold of 60% means replacement is almost always financially viable on a D250 Discovery, given the high residual values of the L462 platform.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery D250 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code AJ20D6 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D250 badge was introduced with the 2021 Discovery facelift, so if your Discovery 5 is 2021 or newer and is the 3.0 diesel (not the 2.0-litre D200), it is the AJ20D6. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AdBlue system, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: AdBlue/SCR system faults are always repairable without replacing the engine. Sensors, injectors, and heaters are all replaceable. Costs range from £400 for simple sensor swaps to £1,500 for more complex crystallisation or heater unit work. However, ignoring the countdown timer is risky — if it reaches zero, the ECU locks out engine start, requiring diagnostic intervention.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery D250 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Discovery D250 AJ20D6 generation. This badge was introduced in 2021, so no older-generation variance applies.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a D250 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare Discovery D250 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: AdBlue / SCR System Failure
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, countdown timer starting (e.g., "Engine will not restart in 500 miles"), poor fuel economy, NOx sensor errors, white crystallisation around the AdBlue injector.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 (sensor replacement, injector cleaning, or crystallisation removal) [PLACEHOLDER]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if AdBlue countdown appears

problems.recommendation: The AJ20D6's AdBlue (SCR) system is its most common failure point. AdBlue crystallisation in the injector or exhaust pipe, NOx sensor failures, and heater unit faults are all well-documented. Unlike the catastrophic V6 SDV6 bearing failure, these issues are repairable but can be expensive if left to escalate. If the countdown timer starts, do not ignore it — once it reaches zero, the engine will not restart. Regular AdBlue system service and using high-quality AdBlue (with a shelf-life check) reduces risk.

problems.title: Cooling System Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, sweet smell, occasional low coolant warning, slight overheating under load.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless coolant loss leads to overheating, which can cause head gasket damage)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; monitor coolant levels weekly

problems.recommendation: The straight-six Ingenium engine has a complex cooling system with multiple plastic coolant pipes and connections. These can become brittle and develop micro-leaks over time. Some early D250s have had coolant pipe replacements under goodwill or warranty. If you smell coolant or the level drops regularly, have a pressure test done. Proactive replacement of known weak pipes (especially the coolant crossover pipe at the front of the engine) is cheaper than head gasket work.

problems.title: DPF Regeneration & Oil Dilution
problems.symptoms: Rising oil level on dipstick, diesel smell in oil, DPF warning light, frequent active regeneration cycles, reduced fuel economy.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil level is rising

problems.recommendation: Like all modern diesels, the AJ20D6 relies on DPF regeneration to clear soot. Short urban journeys prevent passive regeneration, leading to active regen cycles that inject extra fuel, which can wash past the piston rings and dilute the engine oil. Rising oil levels are a serious warning sign — diluted oil loses its lubricity, increasing wear on bearings and the timing chain. For urban-based D250s, shorten oil change intervals to 6,000–8,000 miles and take regular longer runs to allow passive regen.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery D250 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Sensors and injectors are replaceable; crystallisation is treatable
Hose/pipe replacement is straightforward; catch it early
Oil change + forced regen; revise service schedule if urban use
Very rare on this engine; would require specialist work
No known pattern; would be assessed on a case-by-case basis

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery D250? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with AdBlue top-ups recorded (proves system maintenance)
* Cooling system inspected and no coolant loss history
* Service intervals at 12,000 miles or less (JLR's 21,000-mile interval is too long)
* Motorway history — DPF will be in better condition than an urban-only car
avoidIf: * AdBlue countdown active with no proof of repair — this is a costly fix
* Coolant loss with no diagnosis — plastic pipes may be failing
* No AdBlue service history — crystallisation risk is higher
* Engine warning light with NOx sensor errors — often a system-wide SCR issue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Discovery D250's AJ20D6 is a major step forward from the older SDV6 V6 — the catastrophic crank-bearing failure is gone. Instead, you're dealing with modern diesel emissions complexity: AdBlue systems, DPF regens, and cooling system hoses. Buy on service history and AdBlue care, and this is a reliable mile-muncher. Neglect the emissions systems, and you'll face expensive sensor and injector repairs.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery D250 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery D250 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * AdBlue Service History — Has the system been serviced? Any NOx sensor or countdown repairs?
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates a potential leak in the cooling system
* Service History — Oil changes at 12,000-mile intervals (not the extended 21,000-mile JLR schedule)
* Cold Start — Any rattles? (AJ20D6 chain issues are rare, but check for peace of mind)
* Smoke Check — Excessive white or black smoke indicates injector or AdBlue issues
* Oil Level — Rising oil level means DPF/oil dilution problems
* DPF Health — Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more regens
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? NOx sensor codes are the most common D250 fault
